CAT03-064.headline: Narcissistic Relationship Cycles
CAT03-064.subtitle: Idealization to discard
CAT03-064.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans slightly off-balance with one arm half-raised, posture caught between reaching out and bracing, conveying the disorienting pull of a cycle that lifts then withdraws.
CAT03-064.sceneLine: A soft ring of plum accent forms brightens to a warm glow on one side then thins to a faint pale plum trace on the other beside the figure, suggesting devotion that swells and then quietly vanishes.

CAT03-066.headline: Enabling Vs Helping
CAT03-066.subtitle: Where support quietly becomes harm
CAT03-066.figureLine: A single seated clay figure holds both hands cupped open just a little too far forward, posture giving yet uncertain, conveying the fine line between true support and carrying too much.
CAT03-066.sceneLine: Two soft plum accent forms rest near the figure, one held steady and one slumping where it is over-propped, suggesting the difference between helping someone stand and holding them up entirely.

CAT03-067.headline: Enmeshment
CAT03-067.subtitle: When closeness crosses into control
CAT03-067.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with arms drawn tight against its sides, posture pressed and overlapped as if shaped to another, conveying closeness that has lost its own outline.
CAT03-067.sceneLine: Two soft light plum forms overlap so far they nearly merge into one mass beside the figure, with a thin pale plum seam barely visible between them, suggesting two selves blurred past their own edges.

CAT03-068.headline: Power Imbalances
CAT03-068.subtitle: Age, money, and status in relationships
CAT03-068.figureLine: Two clay figures stand together, one slightly raised and broad in posture while the other rests lower and smaller, conveying an uneven footing held quietly between them.
CAT03-068.sceneLine: A long plum accent beam tilts on an off-center pivot beside the figures, one end resting low and one lifted high, suggesting a scale that has never quite settled level.

CAT03-069.headline: Protecting Your Mental Health
CAT03-069.subtitle: Staying steady in a difficult relationship
CAT03-069.figureLine: A single clay figure stands grounded with one hand pressed gently over its own center, posture quiet and self-contained, conveying steadiness held from within amid strain.
CAT03-069.sceneLine: A soft circle of pale plum light holds close around the figure while a denser plum accent mass presses at its outer edge, suggesting an inner calm kept intact against an outside weight.

CAT03-070.headline: When Love Is Not Enough
CAT03-070.subtitle: Why some relationships still end
CAT03-070.figureLine: Two clay figures stand a small distance apart, each turned partly away yet still inclined toward the other, posture tender and resigned, conveying care that remains even as a path diverges.
CAT03-070.sceneLine: A single plum accent thread runs warm between the two figures but frays softly into pale plum strands at its middle, suggesting a bond felt fully yet no longer able to hold.

CAT03-071.headline: The Neuroscience Of Heartbreak
CAT03-071.subtitle: Why breakups hurt so much
CAT03-071.figureLine: A single clay figure sits curled with one hand resting over its chest, posture folded and tender, conveying a real, felt ache held with quiet patience.
CAT03-071.sceneLine: A soft plum accent glow radiates gently outward from where the figure's hand rests, fading to pale plum at its edges, suggesting a deep hurt that the body registers as genuinely as any wound.

CAT03-072.headline: Emotional Stages Of A Breakup
CAT03-072.subtitle: What to expect as you move through it
CAT03-072.figureLine: A single clay figure shifts mid-turn between two poses, one shoulder still pulled back while the other opens forward, conveying the slow uneven movement through changing feeling.
CAT03-072.sceneLine: A series of soft plum accent forms arcs beside the figure, shifting gradually from dense and dark to pale plum and light, suggesting feeling passing through distinct stages toward ease.

CAT03-073.headline: Healing After A Breakup
CAT03-073.subtitle: Evidence-based strategies that help
CAT03-073.figureLine: A single clay figure sits upright with both hands resting open on its knees, posture settling and a little steadier, conveying quiet recovery taking root.
CAT03-073.sceneLine: Small plum accent forms gather into a gentle mended whole beside the figure, a faint pale plum seam tracing where pieces rejoined, suggesting wholeness patiently returning.

CAT03-074.headline: The No-Contact Rule
CAT03-074.subtitle: What the research says after a breakup
CAT03-074.figureLine: A single clay figure stands turned gently away from a closed gesture behind it, one hand lowered in a quiet boundary, posture calm and deliberate, conveying chosen distance held with care.
CAT03-074.sceneLine: A soft plum accent line draws a clean, unbroken arc of space between the figure and a faint pale plum form left behind it, suggesting a deliberate gap kept open to let healing breathe.

CAT03-075.headline: Social Media After A Breakup
CAT03-075.subtitle: Why stalking an ex harms healing
CAT03-075.figureLine: A single clay figure leans forward with one hand reaching toward a small glowing form, posture caught in a pull it half-regrets, conveying a habit that reopens an old ache.
CAT03-075.sceneLine: A small plum accent glow draws the figure's hand toward it while sending faint pale plum ripples back across the space, suggesting a window that keeps a wound from closing.

CAT03-077.headline: When A Friendship Ends
CAT03-077.subtitle: Processing the loss of a close connection
CAT03-077.figureLine: A single clay figure stands holding one hand to the empty space beside it where another once stood, posture quiet and adrift, conveying a grief that often goes unnamed.
CAT03-077.sceneLine: A soft plum accent form lingers as a faint pale plum outline beside the figure where a second shape used to be, suggesting the felt absence of a bond that mattered.

CAT03-079.headline: Ready To Date Again
CAT03-079.subtitle: How to know when you are ready
CAT03-079.figureLine: A single clay figure pauses at the edge of a step, one foot lifted and posture poised but unhurried, conveying a careful readiness to move forward.
CAT03-079.sceneLine: A soft plum accent path begins to brighten just ahead of the figure's lifted foot, pale plum warming into a clearer glow, suggesting an opening that feels safe to step into.

CAT03-080.headline: Starting Over
CAT03-080.subtitle: Building new connections after heartbreak
CAT03-080.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand opening outward toward new space, posture tentative and hopeful, conveying the first reach toward connection again.
CAT03-080.sceneLine: A few small plum accent forms begin to gather and warm in the open space ahead of the figure, pale plum points drawing closer together, suggesting new bonds beginning to form.

CAT05-030.headline: A Partner's Work Stress
CAT05-030.subtitle: How tension transfers between you
CAT05-030.figureLine: Two clay figures stand close, one carrying a visible weight on its shoulders while the second begins to stoop in echo, conveying strain quietly passing from one to the other.
CAT05-030.sceneLine: A heavy plum accent form rests on one figure and casts a faint pale plum shadow that spreads onto the second beside it, suggesting pressure that crosses from one person into another.

CAT05-068.headline: Remote Work And Couples
CAT05-068.subtitle: How working from home reshapes relationships
CAT05-068.figureLine: Two clay figures sit at opposite ends of one shared surface, each angled into separate focus yet sharing the same small space, conveying closeness and crowding held at once.
CAT05-068.sceneLine: A single plum accent surface stretches between the two figures, brightening where they overlap and dimming to pale plum at the crowded center, suggesting shared space that must be carefully divided.

CAT06-002.headline: Dysfunctional Family Roles
CAT06-002.subtitle: How they follow us into adulthood
CAT06-002.figureLine: A single adult clay figure stands still holding the same fixed posture of a much smaller form beside it, conveying an old role carried unchanged into grown life.
CAT06-002.sceneLine: A soft plum accent outline of a small shape overlays the larger figure like a template it never set down, fading to pale plum at the edges, suggesting a role assigned long ago that still fits.

CAT06-003.headline: Enmeshed Families
CAT06-003.subtitle: When closeness becomes control
CAT06-003.figureLine: A cluster of clay figures stands pressed so tightly together their forms blur into one mass, posture crowded and undifferentiated, conveying closeness that has erased individual space.
CAT06-003.sceneLine: Several soft light plum forms merge into a single dense plum accent block beside the figures with no pale plum seams between them, suggesting separate selves folded into one whole.

CAT06-004.headline: Disengaged Families
CAT06-004.subtitle: The hidden cost of emotional distance
CAT06-004.figureLine: Several clay figures stand far apart and turned away from one another, each self-contained, posture distant and quiet, conveying connection thinned to almost nothing.
CAT06-004.sceneLine: Faint plum accent threads stretch thin and nearly broken across the wide gaps between the figures, dimming to barely-visible pale plum, suggesting bonds worn down by distance.

CAT06-005.headline: Family Secrets
CAT06-005.subtitle: How they shape mental health across generations
CAT06-005.figureLine: A row of clay figures stands one behind the next, each holding the same closed, guarded posture passed down the line, conveying something unspoken carried through generations.
CAT06-005.sceneLine: A single covered plum accent form sits among the figures, its faint pale plum glow leaking through to each one in the row, suggesting a hidden truth quietly shaping everyone it touches.

CAT06-006.headline: Triangulation In Families
CAT06-006.subtitle: Using a third person to avoid conflict
CAT06-006.figureLine: Three clay figures stand in a tense triangle, two turned away from each other while both lean toward the third between them, conveying conflict routed through a go-between.
CAT06-006.sceneLine: A plum accent thread runs brightly through one mediating point between the figures while the direct line stays a faint, unlit pale plum, suggesting tension passed around rather than through.

CAT06-007.headline: What Birth Order Does
CAT06-007.subtitle: Its real effect on personality
CAT06-007.figureLine: A row of clay figures of graduated size stands in sequence, each holding a subtly different posture, conveying small differences shaped by position in a family line.
CAT06-007.sceneLine: A line of plum accent forms steps up in even order beside the figures, each catching slightly different pale plum light, suggesting place in a sequence leaving a gentle mark.

CAT06-008.headline: Family Rules
CAT06-008.subtitle: Written and unwritten beliefs we inherit
CAT06-008.figureLine: A single adult clay figure moves carefully along an unseen track, posture self-correcting as if following lines no one can see, conveying inherited rules still steering choices.
CAT06-008.sceneLine: Faint plum accent grooves run across the ground around the figure, some clearly cut and some barely-there pale plum traces, suggesting spoken and unspoken rules shaping the path.

CAT06-009.headline: The Identified Patient
CAT06-009.subtitle: How family systems express dysfunction
CAT06-009.figureLine: A cluster of clay figures stands with one slightly forward figure visibly bearing tension for the whole group, posture singled out, conveying one person carrying a family's strain.
CAT06-009.sceneLine: Plum accent threads from several figures all converge and pool their weight onto one pale plum form at the front, suggesting a system's strain settling onto a single member.

CAT06-011.headline: Childhood And Adult Relationships
CAT06-011.subtitle: How early experiences shape who we become
CAT06-011.figureLine: A single adult clay figure stands with a smaller form resting gently within its outline, posture grown yet still holding an early shape, conveying the lasting imprint of childhood.
CAT06-011.sceneLine: A soft pale plum impression of a small form glows faintly at the center of the larger figure, warmed by surrounding plum accent light, suggesting early years still living inside the present self.

CAT06-021.headline: Parental Burnout
CAT06-021.subtitle: When caregiving exhaustion depletes parents
CAT06-021.figureLine: A single clay figure stoops with arms still extended in a caregiving gesture even as its whole form sags, posture giving past empty, conveying devotion drained to depletion.
CAT06-021.sceneLine: A plum accent glow around the figure has dimmed to a thin pale plum flicker at its edges, nearly spent, suggesting warmth given until little is left to draw from.

CAT06-023.headline: Parenting Guilt
CAT06-023.subtitle: Why so many parents never feel like enough
CAT06-023.figureLine: A single clay figure stands looking down at its own open hands as if measuring them against an unseen mark, posture earnest and self-doubting, conveying the ache of never feeling sufficient.
CAT06-023.sceneLine: A soft plum accent form the figure holds is full and warm, yet a faint pale plum outline floats just above it suggesting an imagined larger shape, conveying enough being met by a feeling of lack.

CAT06-026.headline: Parenting With A Condition
CAT06-026.subtitle: Challenges and support for parents
CAT06-026.figureLine: A single clay figure holds a caregiving posture while carrying a quiet additional weight of its own, standing steady all the same, conveying parenting carried alongside a personal load.
CAT06-026.sceneLine: Two plum accent forms rest with the figure, one held toward a smaller shape and one balanced gently for itself, both lit by soft light plum, suggesting care given while one's own needs are also held.

CAT06-033.headline: Breaking The Cycle
CAT06-033.subtitle: Ending dysfunctional family patterns
CAT06-033.figureLine: A single clay figure steps firmly through a faint loop on the ground, posture deliberate and forward-moving, conveying a repeating pattern finally interrupted.
CAT06-033.sceneLine: A worn plum accent ring on the ground breaks open just where the figure steps through, its pale plum ends parting to release a clear path, suggesting an old cycle deliberately broken.

CAT06-040.headline: Family Estrangement
CAT06-040.subtitle: When cutting contact becomes necessary
CAT06-040.figureLine: A single clay figure stands turned resolutely away from a distant form, one hand lowered in quiet finality, posture grieving yet steady, conveying a hard, protective choice.
CAT06-040.sceneLine: A soft plum accent space opens between the figure and a faint pale plum form behind it, the gap held calm and clean, suggesting distance kept for the sake of one's own safety.

CAT06-042.headline: Parallel Parenting
CAT06-042.subtitle: When high-conflict co-parenting fails
CAT06-042.figureLine: Two clay figures stand apart, each turned toward a shared smaller form between them but never toward each other, posture cooperative yet separate, conveying parenting kept deliberately parallel.
CAT06-042.sceneLine: Two plum accent paths run side by side toward one shared pale plum point without ever crossing, suggesting two routes that meet only at the child between them.

CAT06-044.headline: Blended Families
CAT06-044.subtitle: Psychological challenges of stepparenting
CAT06-044.figureLine: Several clay figures of different sizes stand finding their places around one another, postures a little tentative as they arrange into a new whole, conveying a family still forming.
CAT06-044.sceneLine: Separate plum accent forms slowly draw toward one another into a loose new shape beside the figures, soft light plum filling the spaces between, suggesting distinct pieces learning to fit together.

CAT06-046.headline: Co-Parenting Communication
CAT06-046.subtitle: Tools that reduce conflict
CAT06-046.figureLine: Two clay figures stand calmly across a shared low surface, each gesturing in measured turn, posture even and unhurried, conveying conflict cooled into steady exchange.
CAT06-046.sceneLine: A plum accent form passes smoothly back and forth along a clear channel between the two figures, its pale plum glow even and untroubled, suggesting communication kept calm and workable.

CAT06-049.headline: Dating As A Single Parent
CAT06-049.subtitle: Opening up again after divorce
CAT06-049.figureLine: A single clay figure stands with one hand resting on a smaller form while the other opens tentatively outward, posture caring yet quietly hopeful, conveying readiness to reach forward again.
CAT06-049.sceneLine: A small plum accent glow warms in the open space ahead of the figure while soft light plum stays gathered around the smaller form beside it, suggesting new connection held alongside existing care.

CAT06-066.headline: Sibling Conflicts Over Eldercare
CAT06-066.subtitle: Why they happen and how to navigate them
CAT06-066.figureLine: Two clay figures stand on either side of a shared elder form, each leaning in with slightly different weight, posture strained yet both still turned toward the center, conveying tension over shared care.
CAT06-066.sceneLine: A plum accent weight rests at the center between the two figures, its pale plum shadow pulling unevenly toward each side, suggesting a responsibility carried unequally between siblings.

CAT06-071.headline: Sibling Rivalry
CAT06-071.subtitle: Roots, patterns, and lifelong impact
CAT06-071.figureLine: Two clay figures of similar size stand angled toward each other in subtle measuring postures, one slightly forward, conveying a long-running comparison between them.
CAT06-071.sceneLine: Two plum accent forms sit side by side, each catching slightly different pale plum light as if weighed against the other, suggesting a quiet lifelong comparison between siblings.

CAT06-072.headline: Losing A Sibling
CAT06-072.subtitle: Why this grief goes unrecognized
CAT06-072.figureLine: A single clay figure stands beside an empty space at its own level where a near-equal once stood, one hand reaching gently toward it, posture quietly bereft, conveying an under-acknowledged loss.
CAT06-072.sceneLine: A faint pale plum outline of a matching form lingers beside the figure, held softly by a surrounding plum accent glow, suggesting the tender absence of one who stood alongside.

CAT06-073.headline: Sibling Estrangement
CAT06-073.subtitle: When distance is self-preservation
CAT06-073.figureLine: Two clay figures of similar size stand turned away from each other across a settled gap, postures calm and self-contained, conveying distance chosen for one's own steadiness.
CAT06-073.sceneLine: A quiet plum accent space holds open between the two figures, a faint pale plum line marking the boundary cleanly, suggesting protective distance kept between siblings.

CAT06-074.headline: Adult Sibling Relationships
CAT06-074.subtitle: Navigating old patterns as grown-ups
CAT06-074.figureLine: Two adult clay figures stand together, each beginning to step out of a smaller fixed posture that still clings faintly to its form, conveying old roles being gently outgrown.
CAT06-074.sceneLine: Faint pale plum outlines of younger shapes hover near the two figures while fuller plum accent forms step forward beyond them, suggesting old patterns slowly giving way to a new footing.

CAT06-075.headline: Only Children
CAT06-075.subtitle: What research says about the stereotypes
CAT06-075.figureLine: A single clay figure stands self-possessed and at ease in open space, posture neither isolated nor lacking, conveying solitude as simply one ordinary way of growing up.
CAT06-075.sceneLine: A single well-formed plum accent shape rests in clear open space around the figure, lit evenly in soft light plum, suggesting one complete form standing plainly on its own terms.

CAT07-068.headline: Supporting A Depressed Loved One
CAT07-068.subtitle: Helping without burning out
CAT07-068.figureLine: Two clay figures rest side by side, one gently lowered and the other steady beside it with a hand near but not straining, posture caring and self-aware, conveying support that protects the helper too.
CAT07-068.sceneLine: A soft plum accent glow is shared between the two figures, warm enough to reach the lowered one yet still kept lit around the steady one in pale plum, suggesting care given without being depleted.

CAT08-019.headline: Social Connection And Motivation
CAT08-019.subtitle: How accountability fuels lasting change
CAT08-019.figureLine: Two clay figures step forward in loose unison, each glancing toward the other in shared momentum, posture energized and mutually paced, conveying motivation carried between people.
CAT08-019.sceneLine: A plum accent thread links the two figures and brightens with each shared step, soft light plum pulsing along its length, suggesting connection that keeps momentum alive.

CAT08-058.headline: Supporting Someone Not Ready
CAT08-058.subtitle: When a loved one is not ready to change
CAT08-058.figureLine: Two clay figures stand together, one settled and still while the other waits patiently nearby without pushing, posture accepting and unhurried, conveying steady presence offered at another's pace.
CAT08-058.sceneLine: A patient plum accent glow rests beside the still figure without crowding it, holding soft light plum at a respectful distance, suggesting support that simply stays present until readiness comes.

CAT08-059.headline: Behavior Change In Couples
CAT08-059.subtitle: When partners are at different stages
CAT08-059.figureLine: Two clay figures move along the same direction but a few steps out of phase, one further ahead and glancing back, posture patient and connected, conveying change at differing paces held together.
CAT08-059.sceneLine: Two plum accent forms travel the same path at different points along it, joined by a soft light plum thread that stretches but holds, suggesting partners progressing at their own rates yet staying linked.

CAT10-006.headline: Social Media And Loneliness
CAT10-006.subtitle: The connection paradox
CAT10-006.figureLine: A single clay figure sits surrounded by many small glowing forms yet holds itself curled and apart, posture crowded and still alone, conveying isolation amid constant contact.
CAT10-006.sceneLine: Many small plum accent points cluster densely around the figure yet none reach it, their pale plum light staying just out of touch, suggesting connection that is everywhere and nowhere at once.

CAT10-008.headline: The Highlight Reel Effect
CAT10-008.subtitle: Social comparison online
CAT10-008.figureLine: A single clay figure looks up toward a row of brightly polished forms above it while its own posture quietly shrinks, conveying the pull of measuring oneself against curated displays.
CAT10-008.sceneLine: A line of flawless, gleaming plum accent forms hovers above the figure while a plainer pale plum form rests below, suggesting a polished surface compared against an unedited self.

CAT10-020.headline: Family Technology Agreements
CAT10-020.subtitle: How families can create effective ones together
CAT10-020.figureLine: Several clay figures of different sizes gather around one shared low surface, each placing a hand on it in agreement, posture cooperative and balanced, conveying limits set together.
CAT10-020.sceneLine: A plum accent form rests at the center of the gathered figures with soft light plum boundary lines drawn evenly outward to each, suggesting shared rules shaped by the whole family.

CAT10-028.headline: Parasocial Relationships
CAT10-028.subtitle: One-sided bonds with public figures
CAT10-028.figureLine: A single clay figure reaches warmly toward a distant elevated form that does not turn back, posture earnest and unreturned, conveying a bond felt by only one side.
CAT10-028.sceneLine: A bright plum accent thread runs from the figure toward a distant glowing form but fades to faint pale plum before reaching it, suggesting affection that travels in only one direction.

CAT10-029.headline: Catfishing
CAT10-029.subtitle: Online identity deception
CAT10-029.figureLine: A single clay figure reaches toward a form that holds a different, hidden shape beneath its surface, posture trusting and unaware, conveying connection built on a concealed truth.
CAT10-029.sceneLine: A glowing plum accent form faces the figure while a faint, mismatched pale plum outline shows through behind it, suggesting an appealing surface hiding something other underneath.

CAT12-020.headline: Being A Mental Health Ally
CAT12-020.subtitle: How to show up in practice
CAT12-020.figureLine: A single clay figure stands beside another with one hand offered open at its side, posture steady and present, conveying solidarity shown through quiet, reliable presence.
CAT12-020.sceneLine: A warm plum accent glow extends from the figure to gently include the one beside it, soft light plum spreading evenly between them, suggesting support offered side by side.

CAT14-043.headline: When A Friend Shares Struggles
CAT14-043.subtitle: How to respond with care
CAT14-043.figureLine: Two clay figures sit close, one leaning in to listen with open, unhurried posture while the other opens slightly toward it, conveying a moment of trust gently received.
CAT14-043.sceneLine: A soft plum accent form passes from one figure and is cupped carefully by the other, its pale plum glow held steady, suggesting something vulnerable shared and met with care.

CAT14-049.headline: Families Supporting Recovery
CAT14-049.subtitle: Helping recovery without burning out
CAT14-049.figureLine: Several clay figures gather around one lowered form, sharing the support between them rather than letting it rest on any single one, posture collective and sustainable, conveying care that lasts.
CAT14-049.sceneLine: A plum accent glow is shared across all the gathered figures so each stays lit in soft light plum while supporting the center, suggesting care distributed so no one is drained.

CAT15-002.headline: The Neuroscience Of Connection
CAT15-002.subtitle: Why the brain needs other people
CAT15-002.figureLine: Two clay figures stand near each other, each subtly steadier and more upright for the other's nearness, posture mutually grounded, conveying connection as a basic human need.
CAT15-002.sceneLine: A warm plum accent thread runs between the two figures and glows brighter where they are closest, soft light plum pulsing gently along it, suggesting a bond the body genuinely depends on.

CAT15-004.headline: The Loneliness Advisory
CAT15-004.subtitle: What it says and why it matters
CAT15-004.figureLine: A single clay figure stands slightly apart with one hand lifted toward a wider gathering beyond it, posture acknowledging a shared and widespread quiet, conveying loneliness named as a public concern.
CAT15-004.sceneLine: A faint pale plum thread reaches from the lone figure toward many distant plum accent forms, the gap drawn plainly and calmly, suggesting a widely felt distance brought into the open.

CAT15-005.headline: Solitude Vs Loneliness
CAT15-005.subtitle: How alone time can heal or harm
CAT15-005.figureLine: A single clay figure rests alone in two subtly different ways at once, one side settled and restful and the other slightly hollow, conveying alone time that can soothe or ache.
CAT15-005.sceneLine: A soft plum accent glow wraps warmly around one side of the figure while thinning to a cooler pale plum on the other, suggesting solitude that can nourish or empty depending on how it is held.
